refnamediv.refpurpose: An overview of the Low Discrepancy toolbox.

refsection.title: Purpose
refsection.para: The goal of this toolbox is to provide a collection of low discrepancy sequences. These random numbers are designed to be used in a Monte-Carlo simulation. For example, low discrepancy sequences provide a higher convergence rate to the Monte-Carlo method when used in numerical integration. The toolbox takes into account the dimension of the problem, i.e. generate vectors with arbitrary size.

refsection.title: Design
refsection.para: Low discrepancy sequences are able to produce real number in the [0,1[^s interval, where s is the dimension of the space

refsection.title: Features
refsection.para:
The current prototype has the following features :
manage arbitrary number of dimensions
skips a given number of elements in the sequence,
leaps (i.e. ignores) a given number of elements from call to call
The list of available sequences is described in lowdisc_sequences.

refsection.title: Bibliography
refsection.para: J H Halton & G B Smith, Algorithm 247: Radical-Inverse Quasi-Random Point Sequence, Communications of the ACM, Volume 7, 1964, pages 701-702.
